secondary.name: Ashwood High School
secondary.evidenceNote: The official 2025 report recorded a mean VCE study score of 32.0 and 99.1% VCE/VCE VM completion, with 25.8% of students receiving an ATAR of 90+, supporting a strong rating.

secondary.name: Bayside P-12 College
secondary.structuralNotes: The college operates across multiple campuses and reports combined P-12 outcomes.
secondary.evidenceNote: The official 2025 report recorded a mean VCE study score of 26.6 and 99.0% VCE/VCE VM completion, supporting a below-average academic rating while recognising the strong completion outcome.

secondary.name: Blackburn High School
secondary.evidenceNote: The official 2025 report recorded a mean VCE study score of 31.0 and 98.9% VCE/VCE VM completion, both accompanied by outcomes above the state and similar schools, supporting a strong rating.

secondary.name: Charles La Trobe P-12 College
secondary.structuralNotes: The college reports combined outcomes across its La Trobe and Olympic Village campuses.
secondary.evidenceNote: The official 2025 report recorded a mean VCE study score of 23.6 and 82.7% VCE/VCE VM completion, both materially below the published state and similar-school completion benchmarks, supporting the lowest rating.

secondary.name: Mount Waverley Secondary College
secondary.structuralNotes: The college operates separate junior and senior campuses.
secondary.evidenceNote: The official 2025 report recorded a published median study score of 31, a mean VCE study score of 30.0 and 99.7% VCE/VCE VM completion, supporting a strong rating.

secondary.name: Viewbank College
secondary.evidenceNote: The official 2025 report recorded a median study score of 31, a mean of 31.4, 9.4% of scores above 40 and 99.5% VCE/VCE VM completion, supporting a strong rating.

secondary.name: Albert Park College
secondary.structuralNotes: Separate Year 9, senior liberal-arts, visual-arts and Gatehouse sites; offers VCE, VCE VM, VET and the International Baccalaureate; designated neighbourhood enrolment boundary.
secondary.evidenceNote: The 2025 VCAA-derived result was a median study score of 30 with 7.0% of scores at 40+; the school report also recorded 25% of students with an ATAR above 90 and an IB median of 38/45, supporting a strong rating.

secondary.name: Auburn High School
secondary.structuralNotes: Offers the International Baccalaureate, French Binational and French Partial Immersion programs.
secondary.evidenceNote: The median study score was 31 in every year from 2022 to 2025 and 10.6% of 2025 scores were 40+, supporting a strong and stable rating.

secondary.name: Baden Powell P-9 College
secondary.structuralNotes: P-9 school with no VCE cohort; no secondary-completion outcome rating is assigned.
secondary.evidenceNote: The school ends at Year 9 and has no VCE cohort, so there is insufficient senior-secondary evidence for a strength rating.

secondary.name: Balwyn High School
secondary.structuralNotes: Offers Select Entry Accelerated Learning and early-entry VCE pathways; major campus redevelopment planning continued in 2025.
secondary.evidenceNote: The 2025 median was 33 with 19.2% of study scores at 40+, following medians of 32 in 2022-2024, supporting a top-tier rating.

secondary.name: Box Hill High School
secondary.structuralNotes: Accredited to deliver a Select Entry Accelerated Learning program.
secondary.evidenceNote: The 2025 median was 32 with 11.7% of scores at 40+, and medians were 30-32 across 2022-2025, supporting a strong rating.

secondary.name: Brunswick Secondary College
secondary.evidenceNote: The 2025 median was 30 with 8.5% of scores at 40+, broadly consistent with 2022-2024 and supporting a solid middle rating.

secondary.name: Collingwood College
secondary.yearRange: P-12, with a separate alternative-school campus
secondary.structuralNotes: P-12 college with mainstream, IB Middle Years and P-9 Steiner streams; senior provision is linked to the shared Wurun Senior Campus with Fitzroy High; the VRQA endpoint served a 2023 report.
secondary.evidenceNote: The 2025 median was 28 with 2.6% of scores at 40+, following medians of 26-28 since 2023, supporting a below-average rating.

secondary.name: Craigieburn Secondary College
secondary.evidenceNote: Medians remained 21-22 from 2022 to 2025 and only 0.8% of 2025 study scores were 40+, supporting the lowest rating.

secondary.name: Elwood College
secondary.evidenceNote: The 2025 median was 29 with 3.5% of scores at 40+, below the school’s 2022-2024 medians of 30-31 but still within a solid-middle band.

secondary.name: Fitzroy High School
secondary.yearRange: 7-10 at Falconer Street; Years 11-12 at the shared Wurun Senior Campus
secondary.structuralNotes: Years 11-12 are delivered at Wurun Senior Campus in partnership with Collingwood College.
secondary.evidenceNote: The 2025 median was 29 with 5.4% of scores at 40+, and the school report recorded its best mean result since the shared senior campus opened, supporting a solid-middle rating.

secondary.name: Footscray High School
secondary.yearRange: Years 7-9 across two junior campuses; Years 10-12 at the senior campus
secondary.structuralNotes: Established in 2020 as a three-campus school with two junior campuses and one senior campus.
secondary.evidenceNote: The median remained 29 from 2022 to 2025 while the 40+ share rose to 7.1% in 2025, supporting a solid-middle rating with improvement.

secondary.name: Glen Eira College
secondary.evidenceNote: The 2025 median was 30 with 4.8% of scores at 40+, below its stronger 2022-2024 results but still supporting a solid-middle rating.

secondary.name: Glen Waverley Secondary College
secondary.structuralNotes: Restricted zone and enrolment-management plan due to demand.
secondary.evidenceNote: The official 2025 report recorded a median of 33 and 15.13% of study scores at 40+, supporting a top-tier rating.

secondary.name: Kew High School
secondary.structuralNotes: The school states that it is non-selective; no selective-entry stream was used in this rating.
secondary.evidenceNote: The 2025 median was 31 with 10.3% of scores at 40+, improving on 2024 and supporting a strong rating.

secondary.name: Koonung Secondary College
secondary.evidenceNote: The median was 32 in 2023-2025 and 8.8% of 2025 scores were 40+, supporting a strong and stable rating.

secondary.name: McKinnon Secondary College
secondary.yearRange: Years 7 and 10-12 at McKinnon Road; Years 8-9 at East Campus
secondary.structuralNotes: Two-campus structure; the school report describes it as a non-selective government school.
secondary.evidenceNote: The official 2025 report recorded a median of 33, 12.5% of scores above 40 and top-three non-selective government-school performance, supporting a top-tier rating.

secondary.name: Mount Alexander 7-12 College
secondary.structuralNotes: The 2025 school report states a median of 32, while VCAA-derived reporting lists 31; the VCAA-derived figure is used here.

secondary.name: Northcote High School
secondary.evidenceNote: The 2025 median was 31 with 9.0% of scores at 40+, consistent with 2022-2024 and supporting a strong rating.

secondary.name: Parkdale Secondary College
secondary.evidenceNote: The 2025 median was 29 with 3.7% of scores at 40+, close to its 2022-2024 results and supporting a solid-middle rating.

secondary.name: Patterson River Secondary College
secondary.evidenceNote: The median was 27 in both 2024 and 2025 and 2.3% of 2025 scores were 40+, supporting a below-average rating.

secondary.name: Prahran High School

secondary.name: Preston High School
secondary.structuralNotes: Opened in 2019; 2025 was the second graduating Year 12 cohort and enrolment was still expanding.

primary.evidenceNote: The four published three-year NAPLAN proficiency averages averaged 68.2% for the school, +5.8 percentage points versus similar schools and -1.4 points versus the state, supporting a strength rating of 3.

primary.name: Seabrook Primary School

publiclyReportedIndicators.measure: Percentage of students in Strong or Exceeding NAPLAN proficiency levels

primary.evidenceNote: The four published three-year NAPLAN proficiency averages averaged 85.0% for the school, +2.3 percentage points versus similar schools and +15.5 points versus the state, supporting a strength rating of 4.
